title: can someone explain why this is so controversial
display_title: can someone explain why this is so controversial
wikitext:
[]: can someone explain why this is so controversial?             Quote Post by risu » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:24 pm I've watched all the episodes and movies and I noticed the religeouse symbols and understand the story for the most part(i think) but why is it considered so controversial?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:26 pm Lots of things, mostly depending on who is doing the complaining. For a start, it is generally considered bad form to kill off all your characters at the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:43 pm BobBQ wrote: For a start, it is generally considered bad form to kill off all your characters at the end.

[]:             Quote Post by risu » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:45 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: BobBQ wrote: For a start, it is generally considered bad form to kill off all your characters at the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:52 pm risu wrote: everyone sorta, kinda, i guess,

[]:             Quote Post by risu » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:57 pm back on to my question?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 11, 2007 9:59 pm Because stupid people here Angels and say ZOMG they're fighting a war against God. Then there's the fact that no one can completely agree on NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:06 pm I had always assumed it was because of the two endings. EoTV: Controversial because it does away with all the plot and focuses instead purely on the psychology of the characters EoE: Extremely violent and disturbing. Even though some may say the characters weren't "dead", they were arguably killed for their souls to be released. Thinking of them as not being dead is like thinking that people who are in heaven/hell are not dead. Which I suppose some people may think. I'm agnostic so I don't really know how I'd feel about the situation. But yes, it's meant to be a very psychologically disturbing movie.

[]:             Quote Post by KingSpider » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:06 pm Also, EoE being mindrape, and the series having two endings that make little sense don't help.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:14 pm NAveryW wrote: EoE: Extremely violent and disturbing. Even though some may say the characters weren't "dead", they were arguably killed for their souls to be released. Thinking of them as not being dead is like thinking that people who are in heaven/hell are not dead. Which I suppose some people may think. I'm agnostic so I don't really know how I'd feel about the situation. But yes, it's meant to be a very psychologically disturbing movie.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:16 pm Why? Does it need to be stated that Evangelion was an evening hour kids show? Do I also have to point out that ending of TV series just pissed people off to no end, and then it was supplemented with a movie that put people through an emotional meat grinder? Seems pretty obvious to me.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:20 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Call me simplistic but if you can restore your physical body then you're not dead. Death is permanent.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:21 pm Maggot Master, why do you have a giant penis for a sig? Do you WANT to be banned?!

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:22 pm NAveryW wrote: Maggot Master, why do you have a giant penis for a sig? Do you WANT to be banned?!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:52 pm MaggotMaster wrote: You know though, SEELE themselves call it death, it technically isn't but the process was at least intended to be permanent.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Jan 11, 2007 10:55 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Call me simplistic but if you can restore your physical body then you're not dead. Death is permanent.

[]: Re: can someone explain why this is so controversial?             Quote Post by zephyr72 » Thu Jan 11, 2007 11:19 pm risu wrote: why is it considered so controversial?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:46 pm I thought I mentioned my views on this on another theard of the same topic, but I always thought it was because of not knowing wheather or not EoTV and EoE is concurrent or not? That and the psychological mind-fucks that both the series ED and EoE have probably piss people off... I guess people don't like the fact they have to find their own answers (Ha!) instead of everything being spoon-fed to them. Though it's somewhat good to know the death threats were in fact meant for Death & Rebirth and NOT EoTV as stupid Leaky states. :roll: @ the post above me: GAINAX themseleves stated the Judo-Christian reilgious symbols were just meant to look cool (Pointless Reilgious Symbolism! TM) and would have reconsidered using those had they know it would have been a hit internationally.

[]:             Quote Post by flatulant » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:48 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Though it's somewhat good to know the death threats were in fact meant for Death & Rebirth and NOT EoTV as stupid Leaky states. :roll:

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Jan 12, 2007 3:49 pm Because nearly everyone else here does and because it's not a trustworthy source of info no matter how much you fix it.
